title: [Epic] Separate Immutable Game Content from Mutable Player State
body:
## Issue description

Today `GameTheme` serves as both the immutable content blueprint **and** the source of initial player state via `createRunStateFromTheme()`. Theme data (generators, upgrades, costs, multipliers) is baked into the domain package at compile time and referenced by a simple string `themeId` on `PlayerAccount`. There is no versioning, no runtime loading, and no way to evolve content without redeploying code.

This means:
- Content changes require a full code deploy.
- There is no way to know which version of the rules a player's run was started under.
- Multiple content authors cannot work in parallel without merge conflicts in TypeScript source files.
- Future features (seasonal themes, A/B testing, community-created content) have no foundation.

## High-level requirements

This epic tracks the work to:
1. **Extract** a `ContentDefinition` layer that cleanly separates immutable game content (generator templates, upgrade templates, economy constants) from mutable player state.
2. **Add content versioning** so every content snapshot has a semantic version stamp.
3. **Bind each player account** to the specific content version it was created under, so gameplay rules remain deterministic even as content evolves.

## Additional notes

- The current `GameTheme` type in `packages/domain/src/models/GameTheme.ts` already groups content data well; the refactor is about making it versionable and loadable at runtime.
- The `InMemoryThemeRepository` and `ThemeRepository` port in `packages/application/src/ports/ThemeRepository.ts` will evolve into a content registry.
- No changes to Azure platform choices (PostgreSQL, Service Bus, Container Apps).
- This epic is sequenced **Immediate → Next** because it unblocks content versioning and the campaign system.

### Child issues
- Extract ContentDefinition types from GameTheme (Immediate)
- Add content version stamping (Next)
- Bind player accounts to content version at creation (Next)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Immutable content definitions are a separate, versionable concept from mutable player state
- [ ] Every content snapshot carries a version identifier
- [ ] Player accounts record which content version they were created with
- [ ] Existing tests continue to pass with the new structure
- [ ] Architecture docs are updated to reflect the content/state separation
